hero.title: Dex Digital Sample Library builds open cultural infrastructure artists can trust.
hero.lede: We commission and publish artist-made audio-visual work for legal reuse under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.
copy: Dex is a nonprofit, public-interest library focused on practical openness. We design capture, editorial, and metadata systems so people can actually reuse what they find.
Our baseline is simple: compensate artists, document provenance, publish clearly, and make attribution straightforward in real production contexts.

model.title: A rights-forward production model from commission to release.

cards.title: Commission
cards.body: We scope sessions with artists first, align creative goals, and set reuse expectations before capture starts.

cards.title: Capture
cards.body: We record for downstream value: clean audio, reusable angles, robust notes, and durable source files.

cards.title: Clear Rights
cards.body: Licensing is explicit at publish time. We prioritize plain-language attribution patterns that creators can ship quickly.

cards.title: Open Publication
cards.body: Release pages include context, metadata, and credits so work can be reused by educators, artists, and institutions.

impact.title: Open access is only useful when it is operational.
copy: Dex reduces rights ambiguity for artists, students, curators, and independent creators. The result is less legal hesitation and more legitimate reuse.
We measure success by practical outcomes: releases cited, classes using materials, artist participation, and repeat partner demand for open workflows.
bullets: Commissioned work that remains publicly usable after release cycles.
Attribution examples designed for video descriptions, liner notes, and classrooms.
Long-term catalog access without paywall lock-in.
Rights and metadata discipline that supports archival reuse.

team.title: A compact team shipping production, rights, and platform work.
team.intro: We keep the operating model small and accountable so decisions can move quickly while standards remain high.

members.name: S. SUAREZ-SOLIS
members.bio: Leads strategy, programs, and partnerships. Shapes commissioning priorities and keeps Dex aligned with a commons-first mission across arts, education, and civic collaborations.

members.name: C. CHURCH
members.bio: Directs capture and editorial systems. Designs session formats that translate into reusable releases and supports production training across contributors.

members.name: T. JORDAN
members.bio: Maintains financial and rights stewardship. Implements licensing policy and translates legal structure into practical publishing guidance.

partners.title: Work with Dex on commissions, education, and open release strategy.
partners.copy: We collaborate with ensembles, presenters, archives, labels, schools, and civic institutions that want cultural work to remain reusable and well-documented.
bullets: Co-commission sessions released for legal downstream use.
Host recording days with clear production and rights workflows.
Contribute archival materials with durable metadata standards.
Pilot education modules using openly licensed material.

press.title: Core facts, boilerplate, and media access.
press.boilerplate: Dex Digital Sample Library is a nonprofit open-access catalog of artist-made audio-visual materials published for legal reuse under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.

contact.title: Start a conversation, request support, or follow release updates.
contact.intro: For proposals, rights questions, or collaboration inquiries, contact Dex directly. We respond as quickly as possible, typically within 3 to 5 business days.

newsletter.prompt: Get mission updates, new releases, and open-call notices.

license.title: Reuse baseline
license.body: Unless noted otherwise, Dex materials are released under CC BY 4.0. Credit artists and Dex, link to source pages when possible, and indicate edits.
bullets: Catalog materials are reusable, including commercial use, with attribution.
Dex trademarks and partner logos are not covered by CC BY.
When in doubt, include source URL and edit notes.
